title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search · Graph Lens: Gita Bisect Start Range Paths
summary: A nondeterministic performance regression lies within 2,400 commits, skips unbuildable revisions, depends on generated assets, and may have entered on a merged side branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita bisect start range paths increment in reproducible bisect harness and causal report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Bisect Start Range Paths
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G21 is a theory in a disposable repository. the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set. The learner must predict bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity, run git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts, explain the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID, test this changed condition: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly, and preserve this recovery boundary: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-bisect-start-range-paths — Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the regression entered within the ancestry and path-limited candidate set
git bisect start bad-tip known-good -- src/parser.ts tests/parser.test.ts
# expected: bisect selects a midpoint while preserving explicit good and bad polarity
# observation: the first trial halves 2400 candidates and records the tested object ID
# changed-case: mark an unbuildable midpoint skip and report the remaining adjacent candidate range honestly
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: save git bisect log and reset to the original branch after retaining the regression probe
# transfer-limit: execute real historical code only in an isolated environment with bounded credentials and network
